title: potentilla ovina var. pinnatisecta
intro: Potentilla ovina var. pinnatisecta, also known as the alpine cinquefoil, is a perennial plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, and protect against infection. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as antioxidant, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects. Potentilla ovina var. pinnatisecta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its roots and leaves can be used in topical applications to support skin health and wound healing.
